evidence_text: The paper states that existing agentic coding benchmarks mainly cover limited task scopes such as bug fixing within a single pull request.
source_excerpt: "Existing agentic coding benchmarks, however, cover a limited task scope, e.g., bug fixing within a single pull request (PR)"
note: This is a characterization of prior benchmarks made by the source.
caveat: This is a characterization of prior benchmarks made by the source.

evidence_text: FeatureBench uses an execution-based evaluation protocol and a test-driven collection method that automatically derives tasks from repositories with minimal human effort.
source_excerpt: "FeatureBench incorporates an execution-based evaluation protocol and a scalable test-driven method that automatically derives tasks from code repositories with minimal human effort."
note: The evidence directly describes how the benchmark is constructed and evaluated.
caveat: The evidence directly describes how the benchmark is constructed and evaluated.

evidence_text: The collection method traces unit tests along a dependency graph to identify feature-level tasks that can span multiple commits and pull requests.
source_excerpt: "By tracing from unit tests along a dependency graph, our approach can identify feature-level coding tasks spanning multiple commits and PRs scattered across the development timeline"
note: The statement describes the extraction mechanism used to construct tasks.
caveat: The statement describes the extraction mechanism used to construct tasks.

evidence_text: The first version of FeatureBench contains 200 evaluation tasks and 3825 executable environments from 24 open-source repositories.
source_excerpt: "Using this framework, we curated 200 challenging evaluation tasks and 3825 executable environments from 24 open-source repositories in the first version of our benchmark."
note: This is a benchmark composition statistic reported by the source.
caveat: This is a benchmark composition statistic reported by the source.

evidence_text: The paper reports that Claude 4.5 Opus achieves 74.4% resolved rate on SWE-bench but only 11.0% on FeatureBench.
source_excerpt: "Claude 4.5 Opus, which achieves a 74.4% resolved rate on SWE-bench, succeeds on only 11.0% of tasks"
note: This is a direct cross-benchmark performance comparison reported in the abstract.
caveat: This is a direct cross-benchmark performance comparison reported in the abstract.

evidence_text: Using the Codex agent scaffold, GPT-5.1-Codex with medium reasoning resolved 12.5% of task cases.
source_excerpt: "Using the Codex agent framework, GPT-5.1-Codex (medium reasoning) successfully completes 12.5% of the task cases."
note: This is a reported baseline result on the benchmark.
caveat: This is a reported baseline result on the benchmark.

evidence_text: Each problem prompt explicitly provides interface definitions, import paths, expected behaviors, and requires a directly callable solution.
source_excerpt: "Each prompt explicitly specifies the clear interface definitions, import paths, and the descriptions of expected behaviors, and enforces that the solution must be directly callable"
note: This is part of the benchmark's evaluation design.
caveat: This is part of the benchmark's evaluation design.

evidence_text: Repository environment setup is the only manual step, takes about three minutes per repository, and totals less than one hour across 24 repositories.
source_excerpt: "we manually specify installation commands (taking approximately three minutes)" and "the total human labor required to complete this for all 24 repositories amounts to less than one hour"
note: This evidence describes the manual labor requirement in the collection pipeline.
caveat: This evidence describes the manual labor requirement in the collection pipeline.

evidence_text: The full benchmark configuration uses five pass-to-pass test files per environment.
source_excerpt: "Leveraging our pipelines, we configured the number of P2P test files to five"
note: This is a benchmark configuration detail for the full set.
caveat: This is a benchmark configuration detail for the full set.

evidence_text: Tasks were filtered to include only cases with more than 100 lines of pending implementation, at least 10 fail-to-pass test points, and test files initially committed after May 2022.
source_excerpt: "we restricted inclusion to tasks exceeding 100 lines of pending implementation, encompassing at least 10 F2P test points, with test files initially committed after May 2022"
note: This is a stated dataset filtering rule.
caveat: This is a stated dataset filtering rule.

evidence_text: The benchmark's first version contains tasks created from May 2022 through September 2025.
source_excerpt: "Using this pipeline, we have curated a benchmark with 200 evaluation instances and 3825 verifiable environments, created from May 2022 to September 2025"
note: This gives the creation-time range of benchmark instances.
caveat: This gives the creation-time range of benchmark instances.

evidence_text: Average FeatureBench L1 problem texts are 4818.0 words long versus 195.1 words for SWE-bench.
source_excerpt: Table 3 reports "Problem Texts Length (Words) 195.1" for SWE-bench and "4818.0" for FeatureBench.
note: This is a quantitative task-complexity comparison between the two benchmarks.
caveat: This is a quantitative task-complexity comparison between the two benchmarks.

evidence_text: Average gold solutions in FeatureBench L1 span 790.2 lines, 15.7 files, and 29.2 functions, versus 32.8 lines, 1.7 files, and 3 functions in SWE-bench.
source_excerpt: Table 3 lists "Gold Solution # Lines 32.8" vs "790.2", "# Files 1.7" vs "15.7", and "# Functions 3" vs "29.2" for SWE-bench and FeatureBench respectively.
note: This evidence combines related implementation-size metrics from the same comparison table.
caveat: This evidence combines related implementation-size metrics from the same comparison table.

evidence_text: On the shared-repository subset, Claude Opus 4.5 resolved 74.40% of SWE-bench Verified tasks but 5.2% of FeatureBench subset tasks.
source_excerpt: Table 4 reports for "Claude Opus 4.5" a "% Resolved" of "74.40" on SWE-bench Verified and "5.2" on the FeatureBench subset.
note: This is a direct subset comparison intended by the source to align repository coverage.
caveat: This is a direct subset comparison intended by the source to align repository coverage.

evidence_text: Removing explicit interface information reduced Lite-set performance: Gemini-3-Pro-Preview fell from 10.0% to 3.3% resolved, and GPT-5.1-Codex fell from 20.0% to 16.7% resolved.
source_excerpt: Under "Without Interface" in Table 7, Gemini-3-Pro-Preview shows "3.3 (-6.7)" resolved and GPT-5.1-Codex shows "16.7 (-3.3)"; the comparison baseline in the same table implies 10.0% and 20.0% resolved with interface visible.
note: The evidence supports the paper's claim that explicit interfaces materially affect performance.
caveat: The evidence supports the paper's claim that explicit interfaces materially affect performance.

evidence_text: Providing visible unit tests sharply increased Lite-set performance: GPT-5.1-Codex reached 63.3% resolved and 80.9% passed, while Gemini-3-Pro-Preview reached 60.0% resolved and 80.6% passed.
source_excerpt: In Table 7 under "Visible Unit Tests," GPT-5.1-Codex reports "63.3 (+43.3)" resolved and "80.9 (+20.7)" passed, while Gemini-3-Pro-Preview reports "60.0 (+50.0)" resolved and "80.6 (+37.2)" passed.
note: This evidence shows the measured effect of giving agents ground-truth unit tests.
caveat: This evidence shows the measured effect of giving agents ground-truth unit tests.

evidence_text: The LLM classifier for identifying top-level tested objects achieved 81.03% precision, 89.24% recall, 84.94% F1, and 91.74% accuracy.
source_excerpt: Table 9 reports "Precision 81.03% Recall 89.24% F1 Score 84.94% Accuracy 91.74%."
note: This is a quantitative evaluation of one component in the data construction pipeline.
caveat: This is a quantitative evaluation of one component in the data construction pipeline.

evidence_text: The authors interpret the dominance of NameError in Claude Opus 4.5 failures as evidence that models still struggle with cross-file dependency resolution.
source_excerpt: "Existing agentic coding benchmarks, however, cover a limited task scope, e.g., bug fixing within a single pull request (PR)"
note: This is a characterization of prior benchmarks made by the source.
caveat: This is a characterization of prior benchmarks made by the source.
